findings.description: CRITICAL exposure: /.env is accessible. May contain API keys, DB credentials.
CRITICAL exposure: /.env.local is accessible. May contain API keys, DB credentials.
CRITICAL exposure: /.env.production is accessible. May contain API keys, DB credentials.
CRITICAL exposure: /.git/config is accessible. Source code may be downloadable.
CRITICAL exposure: /.git/HEAD is accessible. Source code may be downloadable.
CRITICAL exposure: /swagger.json is accessible. Full API documentation exposed to attackers.
CRITICAL exposure: /swagger/v1/swagger.json is accessible. Full API documentation exposed to attackers.
CRITICAL exposure: /api/swagger.json is accessible. Full API documentation exposed to attackers.
Sensitive path accessible: /openapi.json
Sensitive path accessible: /api-docs
Sensitive path accessible: /config.json
Sensitive path accessible: /robots.txt
Sensitive path accessible: /sitemap.xml
Sensitive path accessible: /.DS_Store
Sensitive path accessible: /backup.zip
CRITICAL exposure: /dump.sql is accessible.
Sensitive path accessible: /phpinfo.php
Sensitive path accessible: /server-status
CRITICAL exposure: /actuator is accessible. Spring Boot actuator exposed — server internals visible.
CRITICAL exposure: /actuator/env is accessible. Spring Boot actuator exposed — server internals visible.
CRITICAL exposure: /actuator/health is accessible. Spring Boot actuator exposed — server internals visible.
Sensitive path accessible: /_next/static/chunks/

HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is a web security policy mechanism whereby a web server declares that complying user agents (such as a web browser) are to interact with it using only secure HTTPS connections (i.e. HTTP layered over TLS/SSL). HSTS is an IETF standards track protocol and is specified in RFC 6797.
findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to enforce Strict-Transport-Security.

findings.description: Check for differences in response based on fuzzed User Agent (eg. mobile sites, access as a Search Engine Crawler). Compares the response statuscode and the hashcode of the response body with the original response.
